atomic.h(3) Library Functions Manual atomic.h(3)

NAME

atomic.h

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define SHOULD_EXIT()
#define CLIENT_SHOULD_EXIT()
#define ATOMIC_LOAD_BOOL(ptr)
#define ATOMIC_LOAD_UINT32(ptr)
#define ATOMIC_LOAD_UINT64(ptr)
#define ATOMIC_STORE_BOOL(ptr, value)
#define ATOMIC_STORE_UINT32(ptr, value)
#define ATOMIC_STORE_UINT64(ptr, value)
#define ATOMIC_CAS_BOOL(ptr, expected, new_value)
#define ATOMIC_CAS_UINT32(ptr, expected, new_value)
#define ATOMIC_ADD_UINT32(ptr, delta)
#define ATOMIC_SUB_UINT32(ptr, delta)

Macro Definition Documentation

#define ATOMIC_ADD_UINT32( ptr, delta)

Value:

atomic_fetch_add((atomic_uint32_t *)(ptr), (delta))


Atomically add to an unsigned 32-bit value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_uint32_t variable


delta Value to add


Returns


Previous value of the atomic variable


Usage:




uint32_t previous = ATOMIC_ADD_UINT32(&g_frame_counter, 1);
log_debug("Frame count before: %u", previous);



Definition at line 208 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_CAS_BOOL( ptr, expected, new_value)


Value:


atomic_compare_exchange_strong((atomic_t *)(ptr), &(expected), (new_value))




Compare and swap operation for boolean atomics. Atomically
    compares the value at ptr with expected, and if equal, stores new_value and
    returns true. Otherwise returns false.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_t variable


expected Expected value


new_value New value to store if comparison succeeds


Returns


true if swap succeeded (value was equal to expected),
  false otherwise


Usage:




// Try to set client as active (was inactive)
if (ATOMIC_CAS_BOOL(&client->active, false, true)) {


    log_debug("Client activated");
} else {


    log_debug("Client already active");
}



Definition at line 173 of file util/atomic.h.


173 #define ATOMIC_CAS_BOOL(ptr, expected, new_value)                                                                      \
174   atomic_compare_exchange_strong((atomic_t *)(ptr), &(expected), (new_value))










#define
  ATOMIC_CAS_UINT32( ptr, expected, new_value)


Value:


atomic_compare_exchange_strong((atomic_uint32_t *)(ptr), (expected), (new_value))




Compare and swap operation for unsigned 32-bit atomics.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_uint32_t variable


expected Expected value (passed by reference)


new_value New value to store if comparison succeeds


Returns


true if swap succeeded, false otherwise


Usage:




uint32_t expected = current_id;
if (ATOMIC_CAS_UINT32(&g_next_client_id, &expected, current_id + 1)) {


    log_debug("Claimed client ID %u", expected);
}



Definition at line 192 of file util/atomic.h.


192 #define ATOMIC_CAS_UINT32(ptr, expected, new_value)                                                                    \
193   atomic_compare_exchange_strong((atomic_uint32_t *)(ptr), (expected), (new_value))










#define
  ATOMIC_LOAD_BOOL( ptr)


Value:


atomic_load((atomic_t *)(ptr))




Safely load a boolean atomic value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_t variable


Returns


Current value of the atomic variable


Usage:




bool is_active = ATOMIC_LOAD_BOOL(&client->active);



Definition at line 86 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_LOAD_UINT32( ptr)


Value:


atomic_load((atomic_uint32_t *)(ptr))




Safely load an unsigned 32-bit atomic value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_uint32_t variable


Returns


Current value of the atomic variable


Usage:




uint32_t client_id = ATOMIC_LOAD_UINT32(&client->client_id);



Definition at line 99 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_LOAD_UINT64( ptr)


Value:


atomic_load((atomic_uint64_t *)(ptr))




Safely load an unsigned 64-bit atomic value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_uint64_t variable


Returns


Current value of the atomic variable


Usage:




uint64_t timestamp = ATOMIC_LOAD_UINT64(&client->last_activity);



Definition at line 112 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_STORE_BOOL( ptr, value)


Value:


atomic_store((atomic_t *)(ptr), (value))




Safely store a boolean atomic value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_t variable


value Value to store


Usage:




ATOMIC_STORE_BOOL(&client->active, true);



Definition at line 125 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_STORE_UINT32( ptr, value)


Value:


atomic_store((atomic_uint32_t *)(ptr), (value))




Safely store an unsigned 32-bit atomic value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_uint32_t variable


value Value to store


Usage:




ATOMIC_STORE_UINT32(&client->client_id, 42);



Definition at line 138 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_STORE_UINT64( ptr, value)


Value:


atomic_store((atomic_uint64_t *)(ptr), (value))




Safely store an unsigned 64-bit atomic value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_uint64_t variable


value Value to store


Usage:




ATOMIC_STORE_UINT64(&client->last_activity, get_time_ms());



Definition at line 151 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  ATOMIC_SUB_UINT32( ptr, delta)


Value:


atomic_fetch_sub((atomic_uint32_t *)(ptr), (delta))




Atomically subtract from an unsigned 32-bit value.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to atomic_uint32_t variable


delta Value to subtract


Returns


Previous value of the atomic variable


Usage:




uint32_t previous = ATOMIC_SUB_UINT32(&g_active_clients, 1);
log_debug("Active clients before: %u", previous);



Definition at line 223 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  CLIENT_SHOULD_EXIT()


Value:


(atomic_load(&g_should_exit))




Check if the global client should exit. Requires g_should_exit to
    be declared as atomic_t in the compilation unit.




Returns


true if shutdown is requested, false otherwise


Usage:




extern atomic_t g_should_exit;
while (!CLIENT_SHOULD_EXIT()) {


    process_client_event();
}



Definition at line 73 of file util/atomic.h.






#define
  SHOULD_EXIT()


Value:


(atomic_load(&g_should_exit))




Check if the global server should exit. Requires g_should_exit to
    be declared as atomic_t in the compilation unit.




Returns


true if shutdown is requested, false otherwise


Usage:




extern atomic_t g_should_exit;
while (!SHOULD_EXIT()) {


    process_event();
}



Note


This assumes g_should_exit is declared in the same file
  or extern declared.


Definition at line 56 of file util/atomic.h.

Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>
